---
title: "Import Data"
---

## Country Codes

LIS uses ISO-2 country codes to identify the country associated with each dataset. To support this, the `get_countries_*()` functions allow users to quickly retrieve and verify the necessary country codes before loading data.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE,  results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}
lissyrtools::get_countries_lis()
lissyrtools::get_countries_lws()
```

To obtain an overview of the years available for each country in the LIS and LWS databases, the auxiliary functions `get_years_*()` can be used like in the example below.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}

lissyrtools::get_years_lis(c("es", "de", "it", "fr", "cz", "pl")) 
lissyrtools::get_years_lws(c("no", "za"))
```

Finally, with the `get_surveys_*()` functions provide a snapshot on which surveys were used across the series of a given country.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}

lissyrtools::get_surveys_lis(c("es", "de", "cz")) 
lissyrtools::get_surveys_lws(c("no", "za"))

```

::: {style="height: 30px;"}
:::

Survey changes across different periodsmay introduce breaks in consistency, potentially affecting the availability, content, or coverage of certain variables.

<br>

## Variables

All datasets at a given level share the same set of variables. In practice, this means that even if a variable is not available in the original data provided to LIS, it will still appear in the dataset structure, filled entirely with missing values.

In addition to [METIS](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/frontend#/home), LIS provides two separate variable lists, one for [LIS](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/data-lis-variables.pdf), and another for [LWS](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/data-lws-variables.pdf).

<br>

[![](/images/lis_variable_list.png){fig-align="center"}](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/data-lis-variables.pdf)

### Finding variable names

Each variable is defined by both:

<ul>

<li>its <b>name</b> in the dataset</li>

<li>its <b>label</b>, a short description indicating what the variable represents</li>

</ul>

To efficiently explore relevant variables and ensure you do not overlook any related to your topic of interest, you can search for patterns within variable labels. The `variable_labels()` function allows you to do this by specifying a string pattern:

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
lissyrtools::variable_labels(pattern = 'occupation')  
```

For example, a researcher interested in housing topics could search using a different keyword:

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
lissyrtools::variable_labels(pattern = 'resid')  
```

### Availabilty

Before starting an analysis, it is often useful to assess whether the necessary variables are sufficiently available across countries and time.

This can be done using the `variable_exists()` function:

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
lissyrtools::variable_exists(variable = "ethnic_c", iso2 = c('uk', 'ie'), lws = FALSE)
```

In addition to enabling cross-country searches for a given variable, this function also reports the share of datasets in which the variable contains non-missing values within each country series.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
lissyrtools::variable_exists(variable = "basb", iso2 = c("fr", "de", "us", "uk"), lws = TRUE, share = TRUE)
```

### Notes

Some variables are accompanied by notes. These may reflect:

<ul>

<li>

changes in definition across years,

<li>

limitations in coverage,

<li>

the use of alternative weighting variables, or

<li>

other dataset-specific considerations highlighted by LIS.

</ul>

A convenient way to inspect all notes at once is to switch to the <b>Stata Package</b> in LISSY.

![](/images/project_stata_notes.png){fig-align="center"}

```{r, include=FALSE}
source("../src/stata_machine.R")
library(Statamarkdown)
stataexe <- get_stata_machine_path()
knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine.path=list(stata=stataexe))
```

```{stata, echo = FALSE, results = "hide", collectcode = TRUE}
do "../src/lissyuse.do"
```

Once this setup is complete, all notes for a given country series can be retrieved as follows:

```{stata, eval =TRUE, echo=TRUE, class.output = "foldable"}
qui lissyuse, iso2(se) from(2018)
notes
```

::: {style="height: 30px;"}
:::

If inconsistencies appear in a variable over time, or if verification is needed, the `variable_has_note()` function can be used to determine whether the variable is accompanied by any notes.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
lissyrtools::variable_has_note(variable = "basb", iso2 = c("fr", "de", "us", "uk"), lws = TRUE)
```

Care should be taken to specify the `lws` argument correctly, in order to query the intended database (LIS or LWS).
